input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

task_context.question: The Cincinnati Reds record holder for most stolen bases by a rookie in a season is a native of which city ?
task_context.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

visible_agent_inputs.question: The Cincinnati Reds record holder for most stolen bases by a rookie in a season is a native of which city ?
passages: Billy Hamilton (baseball, born 1990) | Billy R. Hamilton (born September 9, 1990) is an American professional baseball center fielder for the Cincinnati Reds of Major League Baseball.  Hamilton holds the Minor League Baseball single-season stolen base record with 155 steals—10 higher than the previous Minor League record set by Vince Coleman and 25 more than Rickey Henderson's record set on the Major League level.  He also holds the Cincinnati Reds record for most stolen bases by a rookie in a season.

1985 Cincinnati Reds season | The Cincinnati Reds' 1985 season consisted of the Cincinnati Reds attempting to win the National League West.  The Reds finished in second place, 5½ games behind the Los Angeles Dodgers.  This year, the Reds adopted an alternate uniform.  Reds pitcher Tom Browning became the last 20th Century pitcher to win 20 games in his rookie year.

Gene Richards (baseball) | Eugene Richards Jr. (born September 29, 1953) is an American former Major League Baseball (MLB) outfielder.  He played eight seasons in the Majors, from 1977 until 1984, for the San Diego Padres and San Francisco Giants.  As a rookie with San Diego in 1977, he set a modern-day MLB rookie single-season record for stolen bases.

Billings Mustangs | The Billings Mustangs are a minor league baseball team based in Billings, Montana.  The Mustangs are the Pioneer League Rookie affiliate of the Major League Cincinnati Reds.  The team has been a part of the Pioneer League since 1948 with a five-year gap between 1964 and 1968, and has been affiliated with the Reds since 1974 (after an affiliation with the Kansas City Royals).  Along with the Elizabethton Twins, the Mustangs affiliation with the Reds is the longest-running among all rookie-level teams.  The team was officially established on November 4, 1947.

List of Cincinnati Reds first-round draft picks | The Cincinnati Reds are a Major League Baseball (MLB) franchise based in Cincinnati, Ohio.  They play in the National League Central division.  Officially known as the "First-Year Player Draft", the Rule 4 Draft is MLB's primary mechanism for assigning players from high schools, colleges, and other amateur clubs to its franchises.  The draft order is determined based on the previous season's standings, with the team possessing the worst record receiving the first pick.  In addition, teams which lost free agents in the previous off-season may be awarded compensatory or supplementary picks.  Since the establishment of the draft in 1965, the Reds have selected 58 players in the first round.

Sam Jethroe | Samuel Jethroe, nicknamed "The Jet"(January 20, 1918 – June 18, 2001), was an American center fielder in Negro league and Major League Baseball.  With the Cincinnati & Cleveland Buckeyes he won a pair of batting titles, hit .340 over seven seasons from 1942 to 1948, and helped the team to two pennants and the 1945 Negro World Series title.  He was named the National League's Rookie of the Year in 1950 with the Boston Braves, and led the NL in stolen bases in his first two seasons.

1871 Boston Red Stockings season | The 1871 Boston Red Stockings season was the inaugural season of the franchise.  They were formed in 1871 by Boston businessman and Ashburnham native Ivers Whitney Adams.  The team was composed of former players of the defunct Cincinnati Red Stockings franchise, who were brought to Boston and kept the name with them.  Led and managed by baseball pioneer Harry Wright, the new Boston team would join the newly formed National Association of Professional Base Ball Players for the 1871 season and finish the year in third place with a record of 20–10.

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.

  visible agent inputs below.
